2	
3	// Note: if you edit the schema here, you must also edit the schema in the
4	// apps/v4/public/schema/registry-item.json file.
5	
6	export const registryConfigItemSchema = z.union([
7	  // Simple string format: "https://example.com/{name}.json"
8	  z.string().refine((s) => s.includes("{name}"), {
9	    message: "Registry URL must include {name} placeholder",
10	  }),
11	  // Advanced object format with auth options
12	  z.object({
13	    url: z.string().refine((s) => s.includes("{name}"), {
14	      message: "Registry URL must include {name} placeholder",
15	    }),
16	    params: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
17	    headers: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
18	  }),
19	])
20	
21	export const registryConfigSchema = z.record(
22	  z.string().refine((key) => key.startsWith("@"), {
23	    message: "Registry names must start with @ (e.g., @v0, @acme)",
24	  }),
25	  registryConfigItemSchema
26	)
27	
28	export const rawConfigSchema = z
29	  .object({
30	    $schema: z.string().optional(),
31	    style: z.string(),
32	    rsc: z.coerce.boolean().default(false),
33	    tsx: z.coerce.boolean().default(true),
34	    tailwind: z.object({
35	      config: z.string().optional(),
36	      css: z.string(),
37	      baseColor: z.string(),
38	      cssVariables: z.boolean().default(true),
39	      prefix: z.string().default("").optional(),
40	    }),
41	    iconLibrary: z.string().optional(),
42	    rtl: z.coerce.boolean().default(false).optional(),
43	    menuColor: z
44	      .enum([
45	        "default",
46	        "inverted",
47	        "default-translucent",
48	        "inverted-translucent",
49	      ])
50	      .default("default")
51	      .optional(),
52	    menuAccent: z.enum(["subtle", "bold"]).default("subtle").optional(),
53	    aliases: z.object({
54	      components: z.string(),
55	      utils: z.string(),
56	      ui: z.string().optional(),
57	      lib: z.string().optional(),
58	      hooks: z.string().optional(),
59	    }),
60	    registries: registryConfigSchema.optional(),
61	  })
62	  .strict()
63	
64	export const configSchema = rawConfigSchema.extend({

72	    hooks: z.string(),
73	    ui: z.string(),
74	  }),
75	})
76	
77	// TODO: type the key.
78	// Okay for now since I don't want a breaking change.
79	export const workspaceConfigSchema = z.record(configSchema)
80	
81	export const registryItemTypeSchema = z.enum([
82	  "registry:lib",
83	  "registry:block",
84	  "registry:component",
85	  "registry:ui",
86	  "registry:hook",
87	  "registry:page",
88	  "registry:file",
89	  "registry:theme",
90	  "registry:style",
91	  "registry:item",
92	  "registry:base",
93	  "registry:font",
94	
95	  // Internal use only.
96	  "registry:example",
97	  "registry:internal",
98	])
99	
100	export const registryItemFileSchema = z.discriminatedUnion("type", [
101	  // Target is required for registry:file and registry:page
102	  z.object({
103	    path: z.string(),
104	    content: z.string().optional(),
105	    type: z.enum(["registry:file", "registry:page"]),
106	    target: z.string(),
107	  }),
108	  z.object({
109	    path: z.string(),
110	    content: z.string().optional(),
111	    type: registryItemTypeSchema.exclude(["registry:file", "registry:page"]),
112	    target: z.string().optional(),
113	  }),
114	])
115	
116	export const registryItemTailwindSchema = z.object({
117	  config: z
118	    .object({
119	      content: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
120	      theme: z.record(z.string(), z.any()).optional(),
121	      plugins: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
122	    })
123	    .optional(),
124	})
125	
126	export const registryItemCssVarsSchema = z.object({
127	  theme: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
128	  light: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
129	  dark: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
130	})
131	
132	// Recursive type for CSS properties that supports empty objects at any level.
133	const cssValueSchema: z.ZodType<any> = z.lazy(() =>
134	  z.union([
135	    z.string(),
136	    z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.record(z.string(), z.string())])),
137	    z.record(z.string(), cssValueSchema),
138	  ])
139	)
140	
141	export const registryItemCssSchema = z.record(z.string(), cssValueSchema)
142	
143	export const registryItemEnvVarsSchema = z.record(z.string(), z.string())
144	
145	// Font metadata schema for registry:font items.
146	export const registryItemFontSchema = z.object({
147	  family: z.string(),
148	  provider: z.literal("google"),
149	  import: z.string(),
150	  variable: z.string(),
151	  weight: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
152	  subsets: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
153	  selector: z.string().optional(),
154	  dependency: z.string().optional(),
155	})
156	
157	// Common fields shared by all registry items.
158	export const registryItemCommonSchema = z.object({
159	  $schema: z.string().optional(),
160	  extends: z.string().optional(),
161	  name: z.string(),
162	  title: z.string().optional(),
163	  author: z.string().min(2).optional(),
164	  description: z.string().optional(),
165	  dependencies: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
166	  devDependencies: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
167	  registryDependencies: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
168	  files: z.array(registryItemFileSchema).optional(),
169	  tailwind: registryItemTailwindSchema.optional(),
170	  cssVars: registryItemCssVarsSchema.optional(),
171	  css: registryItemCssSchema.optional(),
172	  envVars: registryItemEnvVarsSchema.optional(),
173	  meta: z.record(z.string(), z.any()).optional(),
174	  docs: z.string().optional(),
175	  categories: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
176	})
177	
178	// registry:base has a config field, registry:font has a font field.
179	export const registryItemSchema = z.discriminatedUnion("type", [
180	  registryItemCommonSchema.extend({
181	    type: z.literal("registry:base"),
182	    config: rawConfigSchema.deepPartial().optional(),
183	  }),
184	  registryItemCommonSchema.extend({
185	    type: z.literal("registry:font"),
186	    font: registryItemFontSchema,
187	  }),
188	  registryItemCommonSchema.extend({
189	    type: registryItemTypeSchema.exclude(["registry:base", "registry:font"]),
190	  }),
191	])
192	
193	export type RegistryItem = z.infer<typeof registryItemSchema>
194	
195	// Helper type for registry:base items specifically.
196	export type RegistryBaseItem = Extract<RegistryItem, { type: "registry:base" }>
197	
198	// Helper type for registry:font items specifically.
199	export type RegistryFontItem = Extract<RegistryItem, { type: "registry:font" }>
200	
201	export const registrySchema = z.object({
202	  name: z.string(),
203	  homepage: z.string(),
204	  items: z.array(registryItemSchema),
205	})
206	
207	export type Registry = z.infer<typeof registrySchema>
208	
209	export const registryIndexSchema = z.array(registryItemSchema)
210	
211	export const stylesSchema = z.array(
212	  z.object({
213	    name: z.string(),
214	    label: z.string(),
215	  })
216	)
217	
218	export const iconsSchema = z.record(
219	  z.string(),
220	  z.record(z.string(), z.string())
221	)
222	
223	export const registryBaseColorSchema = z.object({
224	  inlineColors: z.object({
225	    light: z.record(z.string(), z.string()),
226	    dark: z.record(z.string(), z.string()),
227	  }),
228	  cssVars: registryItemCssVarsSchema,
229	  cssVarsV4: registryItemCssVarsSchema.optional(),
230	  inlineColorsTemplate: z.string(),
231	  cssVarsTemplate: z.string(),
232	})
233	
234	export const registryResolvedItemsTreeSchema = registryItemCommonSchema
235	  .pick({
236	    dependencies: true,
237	    devDependencies: true,
238	    files: true,
239	    tailwind: true,
240	    cssVars: true,
241	    css: true,
242	    envVars: true,
243	    docs: true,
244	  })
245	  .extend({
246	    fonts: z
247	      .array(
248	        registryItemCommonSchema.extend({
249	          type: z.literal("registry:font"),
250	          font: registryItemFontSchema,
251	        })
252	      )
253	      .optional(),
254	  })
255	
256	export const searchResultItemSchema = z.object({
257	  name: z.string(),
258	  type: z.string().optional(),
259	  description: z.string().optional(),
260	  registry: z.string(),
261	  addCommandArgument: z.string(),
262	})
263	
264	export const searchResultsSchema = z.object({
265	  pagination: z.object({
266	    total: z.number(),
267	    offset: z.number(),
268	    limit: z.number(),
269	    hasMore: z.boolean(),
270	  }),
271	  items: z.array(searchResultItemSchema),
272	})
273	
274	// Legacy schema for getRegistriesIndex() backward compatibility.
275	export const registriesIndexSchema = z.record(
276	  z.string().regex(/^@[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9-_]*$/),
277	  z.string()
278	)
279	
280	// New schema for getRegistries().
281	export const registriesSchema = z.array(
282	  z.object({
283	    name: z.string(),
284	    homepage: z.string().optional(),
285	    url: z.string(),
286	    description: z.string().optional(),
287	  })
288	)
289	
290	export const presetSchema = z.object({
291	  name: z.string(),
292	  title: z.string(),
293	  description: z.string(),
294	  base: z.string(),
295	  style: z.string(),
296	  baseColor: z.string(),
297	  theme: z.string(),
298	  iconLibrary: z.string(),
299	  font: z.string(),
300	  rtl: z.coerce.boolean().default(false),
301	  menuAccent: z.enum(["subtle", "bold"]),
302	  menuColor: z.enum([
303	    "default",
304	    "inverted",
305	    "default-translucent",
306	    "inverted-translucent",
307	  ]),
308	  radius: z.string(),
309	})
310	
311	export type Preset = z.infer<typeof presetSchema>
312	
313	export const configJsonSchema = z.object({
314	  presets: z.array(presetSchema),
315	})
316	
317	export type ConfigJson = z.infer<typeof configJsonSchema>

1	---
2	title: Add a Registry
3	description: Open Source Registry Index
4	---
5	
6	The open source registry index is a list of all the open source registries that are available to use out of the box.
7	
8	When you run `shadcn add` or `shadcn search`, the CLI will automatically check the registry index for the registry you are looking for and add it to your `components.json` file.
9	
10	You can see the full list at [https://ui.shadcn.com/r/registries.json](https://ui.shadcn.com/r/registries.json).
11	
12	## Adding a Registry
13	
14	1. Add your registry to [`apps/v4/registry/directory.json`](https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/registry/directory.json)
15	2. Run `pnpm registry:build` to update `registries.json` file.
16	3. Create a pull request to https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui
17	
18	Once you have submitted your request, it will be validated and reviewed by the team.
19	
20	### Requirements
21	
22	1. The registry must be open source and publicly accessible.
23	2. The registry must be a valid JSON file that conforms to the [registry schema specification](/docs/registry/registry-json).
24	3. The registry is expected to be a flat registry with no nested items i.e `/registry.json` and `/component-name.json` files are expected to be in the root of the registry.
25	4. The `files` array, if present, must NOT include a `content` property.
26	
27	Here's an example of a valid registry:
28	
29	```json title="registry.json" showLineNumbers
30	{
31	  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry.json",
32	  "name": "acme",
33	  "homepage": "https://acme.com",
34	  "items": [
35	    {
36	      "name": "login-form",
37	      "type": "registry:component",
38	      "title": "Login Form",
39	      "description": "A login form component.",
40	      "files": [
41	        {
42	          "path": "registry/new-york/auth/login-form.tsx",
43	          "type": "registry:component"
44	        }
45	      ]
46	    },
47	    {
48	      "name": "example-login-form",
49	      "type": "registry:component",
50	      "title": "Example Login Form",
51	      "description": "An example showing how to use the login form component.",
52	      "files": [
53	        {
54	          "path": "registry/new-york/examples/example-login-form.tsx",
55	          "type": "registry:component"
56	        }
57	      ]
58	    }
59	  ]
60	}
61	```
62

1 --- 2 title: registry-item.json 3 description: Specification for registry items. 4 --- 5 6 The `registry-item.json` schema is used to define your custom registry items. 7 8 ```json title="registry-item.json" showLineNumbers 9 { 10 "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json", 11 "name": "hello-world", 12 "type": "registry:block", 13 "title": "Hello World", 14 "description": "A simple hello world component.", 15 "registryDependencies": [ 16 "button", 17 "@acme/input-form", 18 "https://example.com/r/foo" 19 ], 20 "dependencies": ["is-even@3.0.0", "motion"], 21 "devDependencies": ["tw-animate-css"], 22 "files": [ 23 { 24 "path": "registry/new-york/hello-world/hello-world.tsx", 25 "type": "registry:component" 26 }, 27 { 28 "path": "registry/new-york/hello-world/use-hello-world.ts", 29 "type": "registry:hook" 30 } 31 ], 32 "cssVars": { 33 "theme": { 34 "font-heading": "Poppins, sans-serif" 35 }, 36 "light": { 37 "brand": "oklch(0.205 0.015 18)" 38 }, 39 "dark": { 40 "brand": "oklch(0.205 0.015 18)" 41 } 42 } 43 } 44 ``` 45 46
47 See more examples 48
49 50 ## Definitions 51 52 You can see the JSON Schema for `registry-item.json` [here](https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json). 53 54 ### $schema 55 56 The `$schema` property is used to specify the schema for the `registry-item.json` file. 57 58 ```json title="registry-item.json" showLineNumbers 59 { 60 "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json" 61 } 62 ``` 63 64 ### name 65 66 The name of the item. This is used to identify the item in the registry. It should be unique for your registry. 67 68 ```json title="registry-item.json" showLineNumbers 69 { 70 "name": "hello-world" 71 } 72 ``` 73 74 ### title 75 76 A human-readable title for your registry item. Keep it short and descriptive. 77 78 ```json title="registry-item.json" showLineNumbers 79 { 80 "title": "Hello World" 81 } 82 ``` 83 84 ### description 85 86 A description of your registry item. This can be longer and more detailed than the `title`. 87 88 ```json title="registry-item.json" showLineNumbers 89 { 90 "description": "A simple hello world component." 91 } 92 ``` 93 94 ### type 95 96 The `type` property is used to specify the type of your registry item. This is used to determine the type and target path of the item when resolved for a project. 97 98 ```json title="registry-item.json" showLineNumbers 99 { 100 "type": "registry:block" 101 } 102 ``` 103 104 The following types are supported: 105 106 | Type | Description | 107 | -------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- | 108 | `registry:base` | Use for entire design systems. | 109 | `registry:block` | Use for complex components with multiple files. | 110 | `registry:component` | Use for simple components. | 111 | `registry:font` | Use for fonts. | 112 | `registry:lib` | Use for lib and utils. | 113 | `registry:hook` | Use for hooks. | 114 | `registry:ui` | Use for UI components and single-file primitives. | 115 | `registry:page` | Use for page or file-based routes. | 116 | `registry:file` | Use for miscellaneous files. | 117 | `registry:style` | Use for registry styles. eg. `new-york`. | 118 | `registry:theme` | Use for themes. | 119 | `registry:item` | Use for universal registry items. | 120 121 ### author 122 123 The `author` property is used to specify the author of the registry item. 124 125 It can be unique to the registry item or the same as the author of the registry. 126 127 ```json title="registry-item.json" showLineNumbers 128 { 129 "author": "John Doe " 130 } 131 ``` 132 133 ### dependencies 134 135 The `dependencies` property is used to specify the dependencies of your registry item. This is for `npm` packages. 136 137 Use `@version` to specify the version of your registry item. 138 139 ```json title="registry-item.json" showLineNumbers 140 { 141 "dependencies": [ 142 "@radix-ui/react-accordion", 143 "zod", 144 "lucide-react", 145 "name@1.0.2" 146 ] 147 } 148 ``` 149 150 ### devDependencies 151 152 The `devDependencies` property is used to specify the dev dependencies of your registry item. These are `npm` packages that are only needed during development. 153 154 Use `@version` to specify the version of the package. 155 156 ```json title="registry-item.json" showLineNumbers 157 { 158 "devDependencies": ["tw-animate-css", "name@1.2.0"] 159 } 160 ``` 161 162 ### registryDependencies 163 164 Used for registry dependencies. Can be names, namespaced or URLs. 165 166 - For `shadcn/ui` registry items such as `button`, `input`, `select`, etc use the name eg. `['button', 'input', 'select']`. 167 - For namespaced registry items such as `@acme` use the name eg. `['@acme/input-form']`. 168 - For custom registry items use the URL of the registry item eg. `['https://example.com/r/hello-world.json']`. 169 170 ```json title="registry-item.json" showLineNumbers 171 { 172 "registryDependencies": [ 173 "button", 174 "@acme/input-form", 175 "https://example.com/r/editor.json" 176 ] 177 } 178 ``` 179 180 Note: The CLI will automatically resolve remote registry dependencies. 181 182 ### files 183 184 The `files` property is used to specify the files of your registry item. Each file has a `path`, `type` and `target` (optional) property. 185 186 **The `target` property is required for `registry:page` and `registry:file` types.** 187 188 ```json title="registry-item.json" showLineNumbers 189 { 190 "files": [ 191 { 192 "path": "registry/new-york/hello-world/page.tsx", 193 "type": "registry:page", 194 "target": "app/hello/page.tsx" 195 }, 196 { 197 "path": "registry/new-york/hello-world/hello-world.tsx", 198 "type": "registry:component" 199 }, 200 { 201 "path": "registry/new-york/hello-world/use-hello-world.ts", 202 "type": "registry:hook" 203 }, 204 { 205 "path": "registry/new-york/hello-world/.env", 206 "type": "registry:file", 207 "target": "~/.env" 208 } 209 ] 210 } 211 ``` 212 213 #### path 214 215 The `path` property is used to specify the path to the file in your registry. This path is used by the build script to parse, transform and build the registry JSON payload. 216 217 #### type 218 219 The `type` property is used to specify the type of the file. See the [type](#type) section for more information. 220 221 #### target 222 223 The `target` property is used to indicate where the file should be placed in a project. This is optional and only required for `registry:page` and `registry:file` types. 224 225 By default, the `shadcn` cli will read a project's `components.json` file to determine the target path. For some files, such as routes or config you can specify the target path manually. 226 227 Use `~` to refer to the root of the project e.g `~/foo.config.js`. 228 229 ### tailwind 230 231 **DEPRECATED:** Use `cssVars.theme` instead for Tailwind v4 projects. 232 233 The `tailwind` property is used for tailwind configuration such as `theme`, `plugins` and `content`. 234 235 You can use the `tailwind.config` property to add colors, animations and plugins to your registry item. 236 237 ```json title="registry-item.json" showLineNumbers 238 { 239 "tailwind": { 240 "config": { 241 "theme": { 242 "extend": { 243 "colors": { 244 "brand": "hsl(var(--brand))" 245 }, 246 "keyframes": { 247 "wiggle": { 248 "0%, 100%": { "transform": "rotate(-3deg)" }, 249 "50%": { "transform": "rotate(3deg)" } 250 } 251 }, 252 "animation": { 253 "wiggle": "wiggle 1s ease-in-out infinite" 254 } 255 } 256 } 257 } 258 } 259 } 260 ``` 261 262 ### cssVars 263 264 Use to define CSS variables for your registry item. 265 266 ```json title="registry-item.json" showLineNumbers 267 { 268 "cssVars": { 269 "theme": { 270 "font-heading": "Poppins, sans-serif" 271 }, 272 "light": { 273 "brand": "20 14.3% 4.1%", 274 "radius": "0.5rem" 275 }, 276 "dark": { 277 "brand": "20 14.3% 4.1%" 278 } 279 } 280 } 281 ``` 282 283 ### css 284 285 Use `css` to add new rules to the project's CSS file eg. `@layer base`, `@layer components`, `@utility`, `@keyframes`, `@plugin`, etc. 286 287 ```json title="registry-item.json" showLineNumbers 288 { 289 "css": { 290 "@plugin @tailwindcss/typography": {}, 291 "@plugin foo": {}, 292 "@layer base": { 293 "body": { 294 "font-size": "var(--text-base)", 295 "line-height": "1.5" 296 } 297 }, 298 "@layer components": { 299 "button": { 300 "background-color": "var(--color-primary)", 301 "color": "var(--color-white)" 302 } 303 }, 304 "@utility text-magic": { 305 "font-size": "var(--text-base)", 306 "line-height": "1.5" 307 }, 308 "@keyframes wiggle": { 309 "0%, 100%": { 310 "transform": "rotate(-3deg)" 311 }, 312 "50%": { 313 "transform": "rotate(3deg)" 314 } 315 } 316 } 317 } 318 ``` 319 320 ### envVars 321 322 Use `envVars` to add environment variables to your registry item. 323 324 ```json title="registry-item.json" showLineNumbers 325 { 326 "envVars": { 327 "NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL": "http://localhost:4000", 328 "DATABASE_URL": "postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/postgres", 329 "OPENAI_API_KEY": "" 330 } 331 } 332 ``` 333 334 Environment variables are added to the `.env.local` or `.env` file. Existing variables are not overwritten. 335 336 337 338 **IMPORTANT:** Use `envVars` to add development or example variables. Do NOT use it to add production variables. 339 340 341 342 ### font 343 344 The `font` property is required for `registry:font` items. It configures the font family, provider, import name, CSS variable, and the npm package to install for non-Next.js projects. 345 346 ```json title="registry-item.json" showLineNumbers 347 { 348 "font": { 349 "family": "'Inter Variable', sans-serif", 350 "provider": "google", 351 "import": "Inter", 352 "variable": "--font-sans", 353 "subsets": ["latin"], 354 "dependency": "@fontsource-variable/inter" 355 } 356 } 357 ``` 358 359 | Property | Type | Required | Description | 360 | ------------ | ---------- | -------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | 361 | `family` | `string` | Yes | The CSS font-family value. | 362 | `provider` | `string` | Yes | The font provider. Currently only `google` is supported. | 363 | `import` | `string` | Yes | The import name for the font from `next/font/google`. | 364 | `variable` | `string` | Yes | The CSS variable name for the font (e.g., `--font-sans`, `--font-mono`). | 365 | `weight` | `string[]` | No | Array of font weights to include. | 366 | `subsets` | `string[]` | No | Array of font subsets to include. | 367 | `selector` | `string` | No | CSS selector to apply the font to. Defaults to `html`. | 368 | `dependency` | `string` | No | The npm package to install for non-Next.js projects (e.g., `@fontsource-variable/inter`). | 369 370 ### docs 371 372 Use `docs` to show custom documentation or message when installing your registry item via the CLI. 373 374 ```json title="registry-item.json" showLineNumbers 375 { 376 "docs": "To get an OPENAI_API_KEY, sign up for an account at https://platform.openai.com." 377 } 378 ``` 379 380 ### categories 381 382 Use `categories` to organize your registry item. 383 384 ```json title="registry-item.json" showLineNumbers 385 { 386 "categories": ["sidebar", "dashboard"] 387 } 388 ``` 389 390 ### meta 391 392 Use `meta` to add additional metadata to your registry item. You can add any key/value pair that you want to be available to the registry item. 393 394 ```json title="registry-item.json" showLineNumbers 395 { 396 "meta": { "foo": "bar" } 397 } 398 ``` 399

29	// Derive font values from registry fonts (e.g., "font-inter" -> "inter").
30	const fontValues = bodyFonts.map((f) => f.name.replace("font-", "")) as [
31	  string,
32	  ...string[],
33	]
34	const fontHeadingValues = ["inherit", ...fontValues] as const
35	
36	export type FontValue = (typeof fontValues)[number]
37	export type FontHeadingValue = (typeof fontHeadingValues)[number]
38	
39	export function getBodyFont(font: FontValue) {
40	  return bodyFonts.find((item) => item.name === `font-${font}`)
41	}
42	
43	export function getHeadingFont(
44	  fontHeading: Exclude<FontHeadingValue, "inherit">
45	) {
46	  return headingFonts.find(
47	    (item) => item.name === `font-heading-${fontHeading}`
48	  )
49	}
50	
51	export function getInheritedHeadingFontValue(font: FontValue) {
52	  return `var(${getBodyFont(font)?.font.variable ?? "--font-sans"})`
53	}

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have comprehensive information. Let me compile my findings into a clear report.

## Comprehensive Exploration Report: Compound Components in shadcn/ui

I've thoroughly explored the shadcn/ui codebase and identified the key patterns used for building compound components with React context. Here are my findings:

---

## **1. ACCORDION COMPONENT**

**File locations:**
- `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/accordion.tsx`
- `/apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/accordion.tsx`

**Pattern:**
- Wraps primitive accordion components from Radix UI or Base UI
- Exports 4 main sub-components: `Accordion`, `AccordionItem`, `AccordionTrigger`, `AccordionContent`
- No custom React context (relies on underlying primitive's internal state management)
- Uses `data-slot` attributes for styling hooks
- The trigger contains header wrapper and uses `aria-expanded` state for icon toggling

**Key code structure:**
```tsx
Accordion → AccordionItem → (AccordionTrigger + AccordionContent)
```

---

## **2. TABS COMPONENT**

**File locations:**
- `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/tabs.tsx`
- `/apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/tabs.tsx`

**Pattern:**
- Exports: `Tabs`, `TabsList`, `TabsTrigger`, `TabsContent`
- No custom React context
- Uses **orientation prop** (`horizontal` | `vertical`) passed to root
- Uses `data-slot` and `data-orientation` attributes for conditional styling
- Uses **CVA (class-variance-authority)** for tab list variants (`default` | `line`)
- Different CSS selectors for horizontal vs vertical: `group-data-horizontal/tabs` and `group-data-vertical/tabs`

**Orientation handling pattern:**
```tsx
<Tabs orientation="horizontal">
  <TabsList>
    <TabsTrigger> // Uses group-data-vertical/tabs selectors
```

---

## **3. CAROUSEL COMPONENT (Most Complex Context Pattern)**

**File location:** `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/carousel.tsx`

**Pattern - EXCELLENT EXAMPLE:**
- Creates and uses `CarouselContext` to share state across sub-components
- Root component `Carousel` manages internal state with `useEmblaCarousel` hook
- Custom hook `useCarousel()` for accessing context in child components

**Context structure:**
```tsx
type CarouselContextProps = {
  carouselRef: ReturnType<typeof useEmblaCarousel>[0]
  api: ReturnType<typeof useEmblaCarousel>[1]
  scrollPrev: () => void
  scrollNext: () => void
  canScrollPrev: boolean
  canScrollNext: boolean
} & CarouselProps
```

**Sub-components:** `CarouselContent`, `CarouselItem`, `CarouselPrevious`, `CarouselNext`
- All use `useCarousel()` hook to access shared state
- `CarouselPrevious/Next` buttons use `canScrollPrev/canScrollNext` to manage disabled state
- Handles **orientation** prop (`horizontal` | `vertical`) for layout changes
- Uses `useCallback` for memoized functions to prevent re-renders

---

## **4. FORM COMPONENT (Dual Context Pattern)**

**File location:** `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/form.tsx`

**Pattern - DUAL CONTEXT APPROACH:**
Creates TWO contexts for nested state sharing:

**Context 1 - FormFieldContext:**
```tsx
type FormFieldContextValue = {
  name: TName
}
```
- Created in `FormField` component (wrapped around `react-hook-form` Controller)
- Consumed by `useFormField()` hook

**Context 2 - FormItemContext:**
```tsx
type FormItemContextValue = {
  id: string
}
```
- Created in `FormItem` component
- Shares unique ID across `FormLabel`, `FormControl`, `FormDescription`, `FormMessage`

**Sub-components:** `FormField`, `FormItem`, `FormLabel`, `FormControl`, `FormDescription`, `FormMessage`
- Uses React `useId()` for generating unique identifiers
- `useFormField()` hook combines both contexts plus `useFormContext()` from react-hook-form
- Demonstrates proper error handling with context usage

---

## **5. SIDEBAR COMPONENT (Most Comprehensive)**

**File location:** `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/sidebar.tsx`

**Pattern - PROVIDER + CONSUMER:**
- Creates `SidebarContext` and `useSidebar()` hook
- Provider component: `SidebarProvider` (root wrapper)
- 20+ sub-components consuming the context

**Context structure:**
```tsx
type SidebarContextProps = {
  state: "expanded" | "collapsed"
  open: boolean
  setOpen: (open: boolean) => void
  openMobile: boolean
  setOpenMobile: (open: boolean) => void
  isMobile: boolean
  toggleSidebar: () => void
}
```

**Key features:**
- Manages responsive state (desktop vs mobile)
- Cookie persistence for sidebar state
- Keyboard shortcut (Cmd/Ctrl + B) to toggle
- Uses `useMemo` to optimize context value
- CSS variables for sizing: `--sidebar-width`, `--sidebar-width-icon`
- Uses `useIsMobile()` custom hook from `@/registry/bases/radix/hooks/use-mobile`
- Context value changes trigger tooltip visibility in `SidebarMenuButton`

---

## **6. TOGGLE GROUP COMPONENT**

**File location:** `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/toggle-group.tsx`

**Pattern:**
- Creates `ToggleGroupContext` to share variant props
- Context stores: `size`, `variant`, `spacing`, `orientation`
- Provider wraps children inside the Radix `ToggleGroupPrimitive.Root`

**Context structure:**
```tsx
type ToggleGroupContext = {
  size?: "default" | "sm" | "lg"
  variant?: "default" | "outline" | "ghost"
  spacing?: number
  orientation?: "horizontal" | "vertical"
}
```

- Uses CSS custom property for spacing: `--gap`
- Child components (`ToggleGroupItem`) use context values as fallback
- Supports **orientation** with `data-vertical:flex-col`

---

## **7. CHART COMPONENT**

**File location:** `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/chart.tsx`

**Pattern:**
- Creates `ChartContext` to share chart configuration
- Context: `{ config: ChartConfig }`
- Custom hook: `useChart()` with error throwing if used outside provider
- Injects dynamic styles based on theme (light/dark)
- Uses Recharts primitives with shimmer customization

---

## **8. SEPARATOR & MENUBAR (Orientation Pattern)**

**Files:**
- `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/separator.tsx`
- `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/menubar.tsx`

**Separator Pattern (Simple):**
```tsx
orientation="horizontal" | "vertical"
// Conditional CSS:
data-horizontal:h-px data-horizontal:w-full
data-vertical:w-px data-vertical:self-stretch
```

**Menubar Pattern (Extensive):**
- No context, but many sub-components
- Exports 15+ components (Trigger, Content, Item, CheckboxItem, RadioItem, etc.)
- Uses nested structure: `MenubarSub`, `MenubarSubTrigger`, `MenubarSubContent`
- Uses `data-slot` for all components

---

## **9. DIALOG & POPOVER (Simple Wrappers)**

**Pattern:**
- Minimal wrapper components around Radix primitives
- Dialog: `Dialog`, `DialogTrigger`, `DialogPortal`, `DialogOverlay`, `DialogContent`, `DialogHeader`, `DialogFooter`, `DialogTitle`, `DialogDescription`
- Popover: Similar structure
- No custom context (rely on primitive's internal state)
- Higher-level components like `DialogContent` compose multiple primitives

---

## **KEY PATTERNS IDENTIFIED:**

### **Pattern 1: No Context (Primitive Wrappers)**
- Accordion, Tabs, Dialog, Popover, Dropdown Menu
- Simply wrap Radix/BaseUI primitives with styling
- Use `data-slot` for CSS hooks
- No state sharing needed - primitives handle it

### **Pattern 2: Configuration Context**
- Carousel, Toggle Group, Chart
- Share configuration/options via context
- Child components read from context to customize behavior
- Single context with specific props

### **Pattern 3: Dual/Multiple Contexts**
- Form component
- Separates concerns (field info vs item layout)
- Contexts are hierarchical

### **Pattern 4: Full State Management Context**
- Sidebar
- Complete state management (open, mobile, toggle, state)
- Provider at root, consumers throughout
- Uses `useMemo` for optimization
- Custom hook for accessing context with error handling

### **Pattern 5: Orientation Handling**
Three approaches:
1. **Data attributes + CSS selectors:** `data-horizontal:`, `data-vertical:`
2. **Group selectors:** `group-data-vertical/tabs:flex-col`
3. **Conditional rendering:** `{orientation === "horizontal" ? ... : ...}`

---

## **NO STEPPER COMPONENT FOUND**

Search results show no existing Stepper/Wizard component in the codebase.

---

## **FILE PATHS (Complete):**

1. `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/accordion.tsx`
2. `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/tabs.tsx`
3. `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/carousel.tsx`
4. `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/sidebar.tsx`
5. `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/toggle-group.tsx`
6. `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/chart.tsx`
7. `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/separator.tsx`
8. `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/menubar.tsx`
9. `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/dialog.tsx`
10. `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/popover.tsx`
11. `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/dropdown-menu.tsx`
12. `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/form.tsx`
13. `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/table.tsx`

The **Carousel** and **Sidebar** components are the best examples for understanding how to build complex compound components with React context in this codebase.

Excellent! Now I have everything I need. Let me compile a comprehensive report for the user.

## Summary: shadcn/ui Registry System Architecture

I've thoroughly explored the shadcn/ui codebase and identified how components and their examples/demos are registered. Here's a detailed breakdown:

### 1. **Registry File Locations**

**Key Registry Files:**
- `/apps/v4/registry.json` - Main published registry with all components across all styles
- `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/registry.ts` - Source registry definition that gets compiled to JSON
- `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts` - UI components registry entries
- `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts` - Example/demo entries
- `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/blocks/_registry.ts` - Block components registry entries
- `/apps/v4/registry/directory.json` - Directory of all available external registries

### 2. **Schema and Type Definitions**

Located in: `/packages/shadcn/src/registry/schema.ts`

**Registry Item Types** (from `registryItemTypeSchema`):
- `registry:ui` - UI components (simple, single-file)
- `registry:component` - Components with multiple files
- `registry:block` - Complex components (dashboards, templates)
- `registry:example` - Demos/examples (INTERNAL USE)
- `registry:hook` - React hooks
- `registry:lib` - Utilities and libs
- `registry:page` - Full pages/routes
- `registry:file` - Miscellaneous files
- `registry:theme` - Theme definitions
- `registry:style` - Styles (e.g., "new-york-v4")
- `registry:base` - Full design systems
- `registry:font` - Font definitions

**Core Schema Structure:**
```typescript
registryItemSchema = {
  name: string
  type: RegistryItemType
  title?: string
  description?: string
  author?: string
  dependencies?: string[]
  devDependencies?: string[]
  registryDependencies?: string[]  // References to other registry items
  files: [{
    path: string
    type: RegistryItemType
    target?: string  // Where to place the file
    content?: string  // For JSON-based registries
  }]
  cssVars?: {}
  css?: {}
  tailwind?: {}
  envVars?: {}
  meta?: {}
  docs?: string
  categories?: string[]
}
```

### 3. **Component Registration Pattern**

**UI Component Entry** (from `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts`):
```typescript
{
  name: "accordion",
  type: "registry:ui",
  dependencies: ["radix-ui"],
  files: [
    {
      path: "ui/accordion.tsx",
      type: "registry:ui",
    }
  ]
}
```

**Demo/Example Entry** (from `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts`):
```typescript
{
  name: "accordion-demo",
  type: "registry:example",
  registryDependencies: ["accordion"],  // Declares dependency on accordion component
  files: [
    {
      path: "examples/accordion-demo.tsx",
      type: "registry:example",
    }
  ]
}
```

**JSON-Based Example** (from `/apps/v4/public/r/styles/new-york/accordion-demo.json`):
```json
{
  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",
  "name": "accordion-demo",
  "type": "registry:example",
  "author": "shadcn",
  "registryDependencies": ["accordion"],
  "files": [
    {
      "path": "examples/accordion-demo.tsx",
      "type": "registry:example",
      "content": "... full TSX code here ...",
      "target": ""
    }
  ]
}
```

### 4. **Separate Registries for UI vs Examples**

**Yes, they're separate but combined:**

- **UI Registry** (`/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/`) - Contains only the component implementations
- **Examples Registry** (`/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/`) - Contains only demo components

**Combined at Build Time** (`/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/registry.ts`):
```typescript
export const registry = {
  name: "shadcn/ui",
  homepage: "https://ui.shadcn.com",
  items: [
    ...ui,          // UI components
    ...examples,    // Example/demo components
    ...blocks,      // Block components
    ...charts,      // Chart components
    ...hooks,       // Custom hooks
    ...lib,         // Utilities
    ...themes,      // Theme definitions
    ...internal,    // Internal items
  ]
}
```

### 5. **How Examples Reference Components**

Examples declare their dependencies using `registryDependencies`:
- This field lists the names of components the example depends on
- When users install an example, the CLI automatically resolves and installs all dependencies
- Example: `"registryDependencies": ["accordion"]` means installing `accordion-demo` will also install the `accordion` component

### 6. **File Structure**

```
/apps/v4/registry/
├── new-york-v4/
│   ├── ui/
│   │   ├── accordion.tsx
│   │   ├── button.tsx
│   │   ├── _registry.ts          # UI components registry
│   │   └── ...
│   ├── examples/
│   │   ├── accordion-demo.tsx
│   │   ├── button-demo.tsx
│   │   ├── _registry.ts          # Examples registry
│   │   └── ...
│   ├── blocks/
│   │   ├── dashboard-01/
│   │   ├── _registry.ts
│   │   └── ...
│   └── registry.ts               # Combines all registries
├── directory.json                # External registry directory
└── config.ts                     # Configuration
```

### 7. **Built-in Registry Configuration**

From `/packages/shadcn/src/registry/constants.ts`:
```typescript
BUILTIN_REGISTRIES = {
  "@shadcn": "https://ui.shadcn.com/r/styles/{style}/{name}.json"
}
```

- `@shadcn` is the default registry namespace
- URL template includes `{style}` placeholder (e.g., "new-york-v4")
- URL template includes `{name}` placeholder (the item name)

### 8. **Registry Resolution Flow**

1. **User runs `shadcn add accordion-demo`**
2. **CLI fetches registry item** from `https://ui.shadcn.com/r/styles/new-york-v4/accordion-demo.json`
3. **Parser reads `registryDependencies`**: `["accordion"]`
4. **Resolver recursively fetches** `accordion.json`
5. **Dependency tree built** and all files installed to project

**Key Files in Resolution:**
- `/packages/shadcn/src/registry/api.ts` - High-level API (getRegistry, getRegistryItems, resolveRegistryTree)
- `/packages/shadcn/src/registry/resolver.ts` - Dependency resolution logic
- `/packages/shadcn/src/registry/builder.ts` - URL building with placeholders
- `/packages/shadcn/src/registry/fetcher.ts` - HTTP fetching with caching

### 9. **Block Components Pattern**

Blocks are more complex and can have multiple file types:
```typescript
{
  name: "dashboard-01",
  type: "registry:block",
  registryDependencies: ["sidebar", "breadcrumb", "separator", ...],
  files: [
    {
      path: "blocks/dashboard-01/page.tsx",
      type: "registry:page",
      target: "app/dashboard/page.tsx"  // Target destination
    },
    {
      path: "blocks/dashboard-01/components/app-sidebar.tsx",
      type: "registry:component"
    },
    {
      path: "blocks/dashboard-01/data.json",
      type: "registry:file",
      target: "app/dashboard/data.json"
    }
  ]
}
```

### 10. **Key Implementation Files to Know**

- `/packages/shadcn/src/registry/schema.ts` - Schema definitions (265 lines)
- `/packages/shadcn/src/registry/api.ts` - Public API (344 lines)
- `/packages/shadcn/src/registry/resolver.ts` - Dependency resolution (500+ lines)
- `/packages/shadcn/src/registry/builder.ts` - URL/header building (163 lines)
- `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/registry.ts` - Example registry combination
- `/apps/v4/registry.json` - Published registry (50,000+ tokens)

This architecture allows shadcn/ui to maintain a flexible, modular system where components and their examples are separately registered but can declare dependencies on each other for automatic resolution.

total 8
drwxr-xr-x@  3 timpietrusky  staff   96 Apr  6 21:39 .

1	export * from "../registry/schema"
2

1	import { z } from "zod"
2	
3	// Note: if you edit the schema here, you must also edit the schema in the
4	// apps/v4/public/schema/registry-item.json file.
5	
6	export const registryConfigItemSchema = z.union([
7	  // Simple string format: "https://example.com/{name}.json"
8	  z.string().refine((s) => s.includes("{name}"), {
9	    message: "Registry URL must include {name} placeholder",
10	  }),
11	  // Advanced object format with auth options
12	  z.object({
13	    url: z.string().refine((s) => s.includes("{name}"), {
14	      message: "Registry URL must include {name} placeholder",
15	    }),
16	    params: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
17	    headers: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
18	  }),
19	])
20	
21	export const registryConfigSchema = z.record(
22	  z.string().refine((key) => key.startsWith("@"), {
23	    message: "Registry names must start with @ (e.g., @v0, @acme)",
24	  }),
25	  registryConfigItemSchema
26	)
27	
28	export const rawConfigSchema = z
29	  .object({
30	    $schema: z.string().optional(),
31	    style: z.string(),
32	    rsc: z.coerce.boolean().default(false),
33	    tsx: z.coerce.boolean().default(true),
34	    tailwind: z.object({
35	      config: z.string().optional(),
36	      css: z.string(),
37	      baseColor: z.string(),
38	      cssVariables: z.boolean().default(true),
39	      prefix: z.string().default("").optional(),
40	    }),
41	    iconLibrary: z.string().optional(),
42	    rtl: z.coerce.boolean().default(false).optional(),
43	    menuColor: z
44	      .enum([
45	        "default",
46	        "inverted",
47	        "default-translucent",
48	        "inverted-translucent",
49	      ])
50	      .default("default")
51	      .optional(),
52	    menuAccent: z.enum(["subtle", "bold"]).default("subtle").optional(),
53	    aliases: z.object({
54	      components: z.string(),
55	      utils: z.string(),
56	      ui: z.string().optional(),
57	      lib: z.string().optional(),
58	      hooks: z.string().optional(),
59	    }),
60	    registries: registryConfigSchema.optional(),
61	  })
62	  .strict()
63	
64	export const configSchema = rawConfigSchema.extend({
65	  resolvedPaths: z.object({
66	    cwd: z.string(),
67	    tailwindConfig: z.string(),
68	    tailwindCss: z.string(),
69	    utils: z.string(),
70	    components: z.string(),
71	    lib: z.string(),
72	    hooks: z.string(),
73	    ui: z.string(),
74	  }),
75	})
76	
77	// TODO: type the key.
78	// Okay for now since I don't want a breaking change.
79	export const workspaceConfigSchema = z.record(configSchema)
80	
81	export const registryItemTypeSchema = z.enum([
82	  "registry:lib",
83	  "registry:block",
84	  "registry:component",
85	  "registry:ui",
86	  "registry:hook",
87	  "registry:page",
88	  "registry:file",
89	  "registry:theme",
90	  "registry:style",
91	  "registry:item",
92	  "registry:base",
93	  "registry:font",
94	
95	  // Internal use only.
96	  "registry:example",
97	  "registry:internal",
98	])
99	
100	export const registryItemFileSchema = z.discriminatedUnion("type", [
101	  // Target is required for registry:file and registry:page
102	  z.object({
103	    path: z.string(),
104	    content: z.string().optional(),
105	    type: z.enum(["registry:file", "registry:page"]),
106	    target: z.string(),
107	  }),
108	  z.object({
109	    path: z.string(),
110	    content: z.string().optional(),
111	    type: registryItemTypeSchema.exclude(["registry:file", "registry:page"]),
112	    target: z.string().optional(),
113	  }),
114	])
115	
116	export const registryItemTailwindSchema = z.object({
117	  config: z
118	    .object({
119	      content: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
120	      theme: z.record(z.string(), z.any()).optional(),
121	      plugins: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
122	    })
123	    .optional(),
124	})
125	
126	export const registryItemCssVarsSchema = z.object({
127	  theme: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
128	  light: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
129	  dark: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
130	})
131	
132	// Recursive type for CSS properties that supports empty objects at any level.
133	const cssValueSchema: z.ZodType<any> = z.lazy(() =>
134	  z.union([
135	    z.string(),
136	    z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.record(z.string(), z.string())])),
137	    z.record(z.string(), cssValueSchema),
138	  ])
139	)
140	
141	export const registryItemCssSchema = z.record(z.string(), cssValueSchema)
142	
143	export const registryItemEnvVarsSchema = z.record(z.string(), z.string())
144	
145	// Font metadata schema for registry:font items.
146	export const registryItemFontSchema = z.object({
147	  family: z.string(),
148	  provider: z.literal("google"),
149	  import: z.string(),
150	  variable: z.string(),
151	  weight: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
152	  subsets: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
153	  selector: z.string().optional(),
154	  dependency: z.string().optional(),
155	})
156	
157	// Common fields shared by all registry items.
158	export const registryItemCommonSchema = z.object({
159	  $schema: z.string().optional(),
160	  extends: z.string().optional(),
161	  name: z.string(),
162	  title: z.string().optional(),
163	  author: z.string().min(2).optional(),
164	  description: z.string().optional(),
165	  dependencies: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
166	  devDependencies: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
167	  registryDependencies: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
168	  files: z.array(registryItemFileSchema).optional(),
169	  tailwind: registryItemTailwindSchema.optional(),
170	  cssVars: registryItemCssVarsSchema.optional(),
171	  css: registryItemCssSchema.optional(),
172	  envVars: registryItemEnvVarsSchema.optional(),
173	  meta: z.record(z.string(), z.any()).optional(),
174	  docs: z.string().optional(),
175	  categories: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
176	})
177	
178	// registry:base has a config field, registry:font has a font field.
179	export const registryItemSchema = z.discriminatedUnion("type", [
180	  registryItemCommonSchema.extend({
181	    type: z.literal("registry:base"),
182	    config: rawConfigSchema.deepPartial().optional(),
183	  }),
184	  registryItemCommonSchema.extend({
185	    type: z.literal("registry:font"),
186	    font: registryItemFontSchema,
187	  }),
188	  registryItemCommonSchema.extend({
189	    type: registryItemTypeSchema.exclude(["registry:base", "registry:font"]),
190	  }),
191	])
192	
193	export type RegistryItem = z.infer<typeof registryItemSchema>
194	
195	// Helper type for registry:base items specifically.
196	export type RegistryBaseItem = Extract<RegistryItem, { type: "registry:base" }>
197	
198	// Helper type for registry:font items specifically.
199	export type RegistryFontItem = Extract<RegistryItem, { type: "registry:font" }>
200	
201	export const registrySchema = z.object({
202	  name: z.string(),
203	  homepage: z.string(),
204	  items: z.array(registryItemSchema),
205	})
206	
207	export type Registry = z.infer<typeof registrySchema>
208	
209	export const registryIndexSchema = z.array(registryItemSchema)
210	
211	export const stylesSchema = z.array(
212	  z.object({
213	    name: z.string(),
214	    label: z.string(),
215	  })
216	)
217	
218	export const iconsSchema = z.record(
219	  z.string(),
220	  z.record(z.string(), z.string())
221	)
222	
223	export const registryBaseColorSchema = z.object({
224	  inlineColors: z.object({
225	    light: z.record(z.string(), z.string()),
226	    dark: z.record(z.string(), z.string()),
227	  }),
228	  cssVars: registryItemCssVarsSchema,
229	  cssVarsV4: registryItemCssVarsSchema.optional(),
230	  inlineColorsTemplate: z.string(),
231	  cssVarsTemplate: z.string(),
232	})
233	
234	export const registryResolvedItemsTreeSchema = registryItemCommonSchema
235	  .pick({
236	    dependencies: true,
237	    devDependencies: true,
238	    files: true,
239	    tailwind: true,
240	    cssVars: true,
241	    css: true,
242	    envVars: true,
243	    docs: true,
244	  })
245	  .extend({
246	    fonts: z
247	      .array(
248	        registryItemCommonSchema.extend({
249	          type: z.literal("registry:font"),
250	          font: registryItemFontSchema,
251	        })
252	      )
253	      .optional(),
254	  })
255	
256	export const searchResultItemSchema = z.object({
257	  name: z.string(),
258	  type: z.string().optional(),
259	  description: z.string().optional(),
260	  registry: z.string(),
261	  addCommandArgument: z.string(),
262	})
263	
264	export const searchResultsSchema = z.object({
265	  pagination: z.object({
266	    total: z.number(),
267	    offset: z.number(),
268	    limit: z.number(),
269	    hasMore: z.boolean(),
270	  }),
271	  items: z.array(searchResultItemSchema),
272	})
273	
274	// Legacy schema for getRegistriesIndex() backward compatibility.
275	export const registriesIndexSchema = z.record(
276	  z.string().regex(/^@[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9-_]*$/),
277	  z.string()
278	)
279	
280	// New schema for getRegistries().
281	export const registriesSchema = z.array(
282	  z.object({
283	    name: z.string(),
284	    homepage: z.string().optional(),
285	    url: z.string(),
286	    description: z.string().optional(),
287	  })
288	)
289	
290	export const presetSchema = z.object({
291	  name: z.string(),
292	  title: z.string(),
293	  description: z.string(),
294	  base: z.string(),
295	  style: z.string(),
296	  baseColor: z.string(),
297	  theme: z.string(),
298	  iconLibrary: z.string(),
299	  font: z.string(),
300	  rtl: z.coerce.boolean().default(false),
301	  menuAccent: z.enum(["subtle", "bold"]),
302	  menuColor: z.enum([
303	    "default",
304	    "inverted",
305	    "default-translucent",
306	    "inverted-translucent",
307	  ]),
308	  radius: z.string(),
309	})
310	
311	export type Preset = z.infer<typeof presetSchema>
312	
313	export const configJsonSchema = z.object({
314	  presets: z.array(presetSchema),
315	})
316	
317	export type ConfigJson = z.infer<typeof configJsonSchema>
318

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase structure. Let me create a detailed summary report.

## Comprehensive shadcn/ui Codebase Structure Report

I've thoroughly explored the shadcn/ui component library. Here's the complete structure breakdown:

### 1. TOP-LEVEL DIRECTORY STRUCTURE

**Root Directory (`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504359940-xiqpgi/`):**
- `/apps` - Main application directories
- `/packages` - Shared packages (CLI, build tools, tests)
- `/templates` - Project templates
- `/skills` - Agent/automation skills
- `/scripts` - Utility and build scripts
- `pnpm-workspace.yaml` - Monorepo workspace configuration
- `turbo.json` - Turborepo build configuration
- `package.json` - Root dependencies

### 2. COMPONENT STORAGE LOCATIONS

**Primary Component Location (apps/v4):**
```
/apps/v4/
├── /components/ (60 files) - Website/demo components
├── /registry/ - Main registry directory with JSON configs
│   ├── /new-york-v4/ - Active design system (v4)
│   │   ├── /ui/ (56 components) - Core UI components
│   │   ├── /examples/ (230 example files) - Component usage examples
│   │   ├── /charts/ (70 chart examples) - Chart component examples
│   │   ├── /blocks/ (27 block directories) - Complete page/section templates
│   │   ├── /hooks/ - Custom React hooks
│   │   ├── /internal/ - Internal sidebar component examples
│   │   └── /lib/ - Shared utilities for registry
│   ├── /bases/ - Base/style configurations
│   ├── /icons/ - Icon sets
│   └── /styles/ - Theme style definitions
├── /hooks/ (10 files) - Website hooks (use-mobile, use-layout, etc.)
├── /lib/ (23 files) - Utilities for registry, components, themes
├── /content/docs/ - Documentation content (markdown/MDX)
├── /styles/ - Global styles
├── /examples/ - Demo examples
├── /app/ - Next.js app directory structure
└── registry.json - Generated master registry file
```

**Packages Location (packages/shadcn):**
```
/packages/shadcn/
├── /src/
│   ├── /registry/ - Registry API and utilities
│   │   ├── api.ts - Registry API functions
│   │   ├── schema.ts - Zod schemas for validation
│   │   ├── resolver.ts - Component dependency resolver
│   │   ├── search.ts - Registry search functionality
│   │   └── *.test.ts files
│   ├── /commands/ - CLI commands
│   ├── /icons/ - Icon utilities
│   ├── /styles/ - CSS/style utilities
│   ├── /templates/ - Code templates
│   ├── /preset/ - Preset configurations
│   ├── /migrations/ - Version migrations
│   ├── /preflights/ - Pre-check utilities
│   └── /utils/ (38 files) - Shared utilities
└── index.ts - Main entry point
```

### 3. COMPOUND COMPONENT ORGANIZATION

Compound components follow a consistent **sub-component export pattern**:

**Accordion Example** (`/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/accordion.tsx`):
- `Accordion` - Root wrapper
- `AccordionItem` - Individual item container
- `AccordionTrigger` - Clickable trigger with chevron
- `AccordionContent` - Collapsible content area

**Select Example** (`/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/select.tsx`) - More complex:
- `Select` - Root
- `SelectGroup` - Grouping sub-items
- `SelectValue` - Display selected value
- `SelectTrigger` - Clickable button
- `SelectContent` - Dropdown portal
- `SelectLabel` - Group labels
- `SelectItem` - Individual options
- `SelectSeparator` - Dividers
- `SelectScrollUpButton` / `SelectScrollDownButton` - Scroll controls

**Tabs Example** (`/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/tabs.tsx`):
- `Tabs` - Root with orientation control
- `TabsList` - Tab list container (with CVA variants)
- `TabsTrigger` - Individual tab button
- `TabsContent` - Content panel

**Collapsible Example** (`/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/collapsible.tsx`) - Simple pattern:
- `Collapsible` - Root
- `CollapsibleTrigger` - Header button
- `CollapsibleContent` - Content area

**Key Patterns:**
- All wrap Radix UI primitives (except custom-built ones)
- Use `data-slot` attributes for CSS targeting
- Export all sub-components as named exports
- Use `cn()` utility for className merging
- Most use `class-variance-authority` (CVA) for variants

### 4. REGISTRY AND CONFIGURATION FILES

**Master Registry Files:**
- `/apps/v4/registry.json` (5,518 lines) - Complete flattened registry of all components
  - Includes: name, type, dependencies, files, cssVars, tailwind config
  - References: `registry/new-york-v4/ui/*.tsx` paths
  
- `/apps/v4/registry/directory.json` (1,172 lines) - Component directory/search index

**Registry Definition Files:**
- `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts` - UI components registry (56 items)
- `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts` - Examples registry (230+ items)
- `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/charts/_registry.ts` - Chart examples registry (70 items)
- `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/blocks/_registry.ts` - Block templates registry (27 items)
- `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/hooks/_registry.ts` - Hook registry
- `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/lib/_registry.ts` - Utility registry
- `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/internal/_registry.ts` - Internal component registry

**Schema Definition:**
- `/packages/shadcn/src/registry/schema.ts` - Zod validation schemas
  - `registryItemSchema` - Individual component structure
  - `registrySchema` - Full registry structure
  - Type definitions: `RegistryItem`, `Registry`
  - Supported types: `registry:ui`, `registry:component`, `registry:block`, `registry:hook`, `registry:page`, `registry:file`, `registry:theme`, `registry:style`, `registry:example`, `registry:font`, etc.

### 5. DEMO AND EXAMPLE FILES STRUCTURE

**Example Files Location:** `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/`
- 230+ individual example components
- Named pattern: `[component-name]-[variant].tsx`
- Examples: `accordion-demo.tsx`, `button-default.tsx`, `button-ghost.tsx`, etc.
- Each example shows a specific use case or variant

**Base Examples:** `/apps/v4/examples/base/` and `/apps/v4/examples/radix/`
- 435+ example files per variant
- Follow same naming convention

**Chart Examples:** `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/charts/`
- 70 chart examples
- Covers: Area, Bar, Line, Pie, Donut, Radar charts
- Variants: default, interactive, stacked, labeled, colored, etc.

**Block Templates:** `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/blocks/`
- 27 complete page/section templates
- Structure: Each block has `page.tsx` + `components/` subdirectory
- Examples:
  - `/dashboard-01/` - Dashboard layout with `components/`
  - `/sidebar-01/` through `/sidebar-16/` - Sidebar variants
  - `/login-01/` through `/login-05/` - Login form templates
  - `/signup-01/` through `/signup-05/` - Signup form templates
- Internal components: `app-sidebar.tsx`, `search-form.tsx`, `version-switcher.tsx`, etc.

### 6. COMPONENT STATISTICS

- **Core UI Components:** 56 components
- **Example Variations:** 230+ examples
- **Chart Examples:** 70 chart variants
- **Block Templates:** 27 complete templates
- **Custom Hooks:** 10 website hooks + 1 registry hook
- **Total Files:** 458+ TypeScript files in registry directory
- **CLI/Registry Package:** 28+ files

### 7. KEY UTILITY AND CONFIGURATION FILES

**Registry API & Utilities:** `/packages/shadcn/src/registry/`
- `api.ts` - High-level API functions
- `resolver.ts` - Dependency resolution (20KB)
- `fetcher.ts` - Remote component fetching
- `search.ts` - Search functionality
- `utils.ts` - Helper utilities
- `builder.ts` - Registry building
- `constants.ts` - Constants and defaults
- `parser.ts` - Component parsing
- `validator.ts` - Validation logic
- `config.ts` - Configuration handling
- `namespaces.ts` - Registry namespaces
- `errors.ts` - Error definitions

**Website Utilities:** `/apps/v4/lib/`
- `registry.ts` - Registry helpers
- `components.ts` - Component utilities
- `colors.ts` - Color management
- `fonts.ts` - Font configuration
- `blocks.ts` - Block management
- `themes.ts` - Theme definitions
- `highlight-code.ts` - Code syntax highlighting
- `format-code.ts` - Code formatting
- And 15 more utility files

**Documentation:** `/apps/v4/content/docs/`
- Markdown/MDX documentation files
- Subdirectories: `components/`, `installation/`, `dark-mode/`, `forms/`, `changelog/`, `registry/`, `rtl/`, `(root)/`

### 8. KEY ENTRY POINTS & CONFIGURATION FILES

- **CLI Package Root:** `/packages/shadcn/src/index.ts`
- **Schema Export:** `/packages/shadcn/src/schema/index.ts` → exports from `/registry/schema.ts`
- **Website Config:** `/apps/v4/components.json` - Tailwind/component path config
- **Source Config:** `/apps/v4/source.config.ts` - Documentation source configuration
- **Next Config:** `/apps/v4/next.config.mjs` - Next.js build configuration
- **TypeScript Config:** `/apps/v4/tsconfig.json`, `/packages/shadcn/tsconfig.json`

### 9. DESIGN PATTERNS OBSERVED

1. **Radix UI Wrapping:** Most compound components wrap Radix UI primitives with styled variants
2. **Data Slot Attributes:** Components use `data-slot="component-name"` for CSS scoping
3. **CVA Usage:** Class-variance-authority for variant management
4. **Sub-component Exports:** All sub-parts exported as named exports from single file
5. **Registry Metadata:** Components register dependencies, file paths, and CSS variables
6. **Multi-layer Organization:** UI components → Examples → Blocks (increasing complexity)
7. **Monorepo Structure:** Apps (website) + Packages (CLI/library) separation

This comprehensive structure enables:
- Consistent component discovery
- Automatic dependency resolution
- Copy-paste component installation
- Rich documentation with live examples
- Extensibility through registries

- **CLI/Registry Package:** 28+ files

### 7. KEY UTILITY AND CONFIGURATION FILES

**Registry API & Utilities:** `/packages/shadcn/src/registry/`
- `api.ts` - High-level API functions
- `resolver.ts` - Dependency resolution (20KB)
- `fetcher.ts` - Remote component fetching
- `search.ts` - Search functionality
- `utils.ts` - Helper utilities
- `builder.ts` - Registry building
- `constants.ts` - Constants and defaults
- `parser.ts` - Component parsing
- `validator.ts` - Validation logic
- `config.ts` - Configuration handling
- `namespaces.ts` - Registry namespaces
- `errors.ts` - Error definitions

**Website Utilities:** `/apps/v4/lib/`
- `registry.ts` - Registry helpers
- `components.ts` - Component utilities
- `colors.ts` - Color management
- `fonts.ts` - Font configuration
- `blocks.ts` - Block management
- `themes.ts` - Theme definitions
- `highlight-code.ts` - Code syntax highlighting
- `format-code.ts` - Code formatting
- And 15 more utility files

**Documentation:** `/apps/v4/content/docs/`
- Markdown/MDX documentation files
- Subdirectories: `components/`, `installation/`, `dark-mode/`, `forms/`, `changelog/`, `registry/`, `rtl/`, `(root)/`

### 8. KEY ENTRY POINTS & CONFIGURATION FILES

- **CLI Package Root:** `/packages/shadcn/src/index.ts`
- **Schema Export:** `/packages/shadcn/src/schema/index.ts` → exports from `/registry/schema.ts`
- **Website Config:** `/apps/v4/components.json` - Tailwind/component path config
- **Source Config:** `/apps/v4/source.config.ts` - Documentation source configuration
- **Next Config:** `/apps/v4/next.config.mjs` - Next.js build configuration
- **TypeScript Config:** `/apps/v4/tsconfig.json`, `/packages/shadcn/tsconfig.json`

### 9. DESIGN PATTERNS OBSERVED

1. **Radix UI Wrapping:** Most compound components wrap Radix UI primitives with styled variants
2. **Data Slot Attributes:** Components use `data-slot="component-name"` for CSS scoping
3. **CVA Usage:** Class-variance-authority for variant management
4. **Sub-component Exports:** All sub-parts exported as named exports from single file
5. **Registry Metadata:** Components register dependencies, file paths, and CSS variables
6. **Multi-layer Organization:** UI components → Examples → Blocks (increasing complexity)
7. **Monorepo Structure:** Apps (website) + Packages (CLI/library) separation

This comprehensive structure enables:
- Consistent component discovery
- Automatic dependency resolution
- Copy-paste component installation
- Rich documentation with live examples
- Extensibility through registries

Key features include advanced processing capabilities, and an intuitive user interface designed for both beginners and experts.

6	
7	import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"
8	import { toggleVariants } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/toggle"
9	
10	const ToggleGroupContext = React.createContext<
11	  VariantProps<typeof toggleVariants> & {
12	    spacing?: number
13	  }
14	>({
15	  size: "default",
16	  variant: "default",
17	  spacing: 0,
18	})
19	
20	function ToggleGroup({
21	  className,
22	  variant,
23	  size,
24	  spacing = 0,
25	  children,
26	  ...props
27	}: React.ComponentProps<typeof ToggleGroupPrimitive.Root> &
28	  VariantProps<typeof toggleVariants> & {
29	    spacing?: number
30	  }) {
31	  return (
32	    <ToggleGroupPrimitive.Root
33	      data-slot="toggle-group"
34	      data-variant={variant}
35	      data-size={size}
36	      data-spacing={spacing}
37	      style={{ "--gap": spacing } as React.CSSProperties}
38	      className={cn(
39	        "group/toggle-group flex w-fit items-center gap-[--spacing(var(--gap))] rounded-md data-[spacing=default]:data-[variant=outline]:shadow-xs",
40	        className
41	      )}
42	      {...props}
43	    >
44	      <ToggleGroupContext.Provider value={{ variant, size, spacing }}>
45	        {children}
46	      </ToggleGroupContext.Provider>
47	    </ToggleGroupPrimitive.Root>
48	  )
49	}
50	
51	function ToggleGroupItem({
52	  className,
53	  children,
54	  variant,
55	  size,
56	  ...props
57	}: React.ComponentProps<typeof ToggleGroupPrimitive.Item> &
58	  VariantProps<typeof toggleVariants>) {
59	  const context = React.useContext(ToggleGroupContext)
60	
61	  return (
62	    <ToggleGroupPrimitive.Item
63	      data-slot="toggle-group-item"
64	      data-variant={context.variant || variant}
65	      data-size={context.size || size}
66	      data-spacing={context.spacing}
67	      className={cn(
68	        toggleVariants({
69	          variant: context.variant || variant,
70	          size: context.size || size,
71	        }),
72	        "w-auto min-w-0 shrink-0 px-3 focus:z-10 focus-visible:z-10",
73	        "data-[spacing=0]:rounded-none data-[spacing=0]:shadow-none data-[spacing=0]:first:rounded-l-md data-[spacing=0]:last:rounded-r-md data-[spacing=0]:data-[variant=outline]:border-l-0 data-[spacing=0]:data-[variant=outline]:first:border-l",
74	        className
75	      )}
76	      {...props}
77	    >
78	      {children}
79	    </ToggleGroupPrimitive.Item>
80	  )
81	}
82	
83	export { ToggleGroup, ToggleGroupItem }

input.prompt:
I need to design a new "Stepper" compound component for the shadcn/ui component library. Here's all the context I've gathered: ## GOAL Create a Stepper compound component for multi-step wizards/onboarding flows with: - Sub-components: container, step items, clickable triggers, titles, descriptions, separators between steps, and step content panels - Horizontal and vertical orientation support - React context to share state between sub-components - A demo example - Registry entries for both the component and the example - NO existing files should be modified — only new files created and registry entries appended ## CODEBASE PATTERNS ### File locations - UI components go in: `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/` - Example files go in: `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/` - UI registry: `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts` (array of items) - Examples registry: `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts` (array of items) - Combined registry: `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/registry.ts` (imports and merges — does NOT need modification since it already imports `...ui` and `...examples`) ### Component pattern (from carousel.tsx — best reference for custom context compound component) ```tsx "use client" import * as React from "react" import { cn } from "@/lib/utils" // 1. Type definitions type CarouselProps = { orientation?: "horizontal" | "vertical" // ... } type CarouselContextProps = { /* shared state */ } & CarouselProps // 2. Context creation const CarouselContext = React.createContext(null) // 3. Custom hook with error handling function useCarousel() { const context = React.useContext(CarouselContext) if (!context) { throw new Error("useCarousel must be used within a ") } return context } // 4. Root component wraps children with context provider function Carousel({ orientation = "horizontal", className, children, ...props }: ...) { return (
{children}
) } // 5. Sub-components consume context via hook function CarouselContent({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div">) { const { carouselRef, orientation } = useCarousel() return
} // 6. Named exports at bottom export { Carousel, CarouselContent, CarouselItem, ... } ``` ### Key conventions observed: - "use client" directive at top - `data-slot="component-name"` on every component's root element - `cn()` from `@/lib/utils` for className merging - `React.ComponentProps<"div">` for extending native element props - function declarations (not arrow functions) for components - Context value is `null` by default, checked in custom hook - Orientation handled via conditional classNames: `orientation === "horizontal" ? "..." : "..."` - No external dependencies needed for custom compound components (just React + cn) ### Registry entry pattern for UI component: ```ts { name: "stepper", type: "registry:ui", files: [{ path: "ui/stepper.tsx", type: "registry:ui" }] } ``` ### Registry entry pattern for example: ```ts { name: "stepper-demo", type: "registry:example", registryDependencies: ["stepper"], files: [{ path: "examples/stepper-demo.tsx", type: "registry:example" }] } ``` ### Demo file pattern (from accordion-demo.tsx): ```tsx import { Component, SubComponent } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/component" export default function ComponentDemo() { return ( /* usage example */ ) } ``` ## DESIGN REQUEST Please design the complete implementation plan including: 1. **Stepper context type** — what state should be shared (activeStep, orientation, onStepChange, etc.) 2. **Sub-component list** with their roles, props, and data-slot values 3. **API design** — how the component is used (show the intended JSX structure) 4. **Orientation handling** — how horizontal vs vertical layouts work 5. **Step state** — how active/completed/upcoming steps are tracked and styled 6. **File list** — exactly which files to create and which registry files to append to 7. **Demo content** — what the stepper-demo.tsx should show Consider these design decisions: - Steps should be controlled (activeStep prop) or optionally uncontrolled (defaultValue) - Clicking a trigger should navigate to that step - Steps should show completed/active/upcoming visual states - Separator should visually connect steps and optionally show progress - Content panels should show/hide based on active step - The component should be accessible (aria attributes, keyboard navigation)

Good -- no existing stepper files. Now I have all the information needed to produce a thorough design plan. --- ## Stepper Component -- Complete Implementation Plan ### 1. Stepper Context Type The `StepperContextProps` will share the following state across all sub-components: ```ts type StepperProps = { activeStep?: number // controlled active step (0-indexed) defaultValue?: number // uncontrolled default (0-indexed), defaults to 0 onStepChange?: (step: number) => void // callback when step changes orientation?: "horizontal" | "vertical" linear?: boolean // if true, only completed+1 step is clickable (prevents skipping) } type StepperContextProps = { activeStep: number totalSteps: number orientation: "horizontal" | "vertical" linear: boolean goToStep: (step: number) => void isStepComplete: (step: number) => boolean isStepActive: (step: number) => boolean getStepState: (step: number) => "completed" | "active" | "upcoming" } ``` The controlled/uncontrolled pattern follows the same approach used in `SidebarProvider` (lines 56-89 of sidebar.tsx): - Accept `activeStep` (controlled) and `defaultValue` (uncontrolled, defaults to 0). - Internal state `_activeStep` via `React.useState(defaultValue ?? 0)`. - Resolved as: `const activeStep = activeStepProp ?? _activeStep`. - `goToStep` calls `onStepChange` if provided, and updates internal state if uncontrolled. The `totalSteps` count will be tracked by having `StepperItem` register itself on mount via a ref-based counter or, more simply, by counting children. The cleanest approach: the `Stepper` root component counts `StepperItem` children using `React.Children.count` (or better, a registration pattern). However, the simplest and most robust approach matching codebase conventions: use a second, nested `StepItemContext` that passes each item's `step` index, and have `Stepper` accept a `totalSteps` count derived internally from a ref that increments as items mount. Actually, the simplest idiomatic design: each `StepperItem` receives a `step` prop (0-indexed number), and the consumer provides it. This is explicit, avoids magic, and matches how `AccordionItem` receives a `value` prop. The context does not need `totalSteps` -- each sub-component just compares its own `step` number against `activeStep`. **Revised context:** ```ts type StepperContextProps = { activeStep: number orientation: "horizontal" | "vertical" linear: boolean goToStep: (step: number) => void } ``` Each `StepperItem` creates a nested `StepItemContext` providing its `step` number so that child sub-components (`StepperTrigger`, `StepperTitle`, etc.) can derive their own state (`completed | active | upcoming`) by comparing `step` against `activeStep` from the parent context. ```ts type StepItemContextProps = { step: number state: "completed" | "active" | "upcoming" disabled: boolean } ``` ### 2. Sub-component List | Component | Element | data-slot | Role | |-----------|---------|-----------|------| | `Stepper` | `div` | `stepper` | Root container. Provides `StepperContext`. Manages active step state. | | `StepperItem` | `div` | `stepper-item` | Wraps a single step. Provides `StepItemContext` with its index. Exposes `data-state="completed|active|upcoming"`. | | `StepperTrigger` | `button` | `stepper-trigger` | Clickable circle/indicator for a step. Shows step number or check icon. Handles click to navigate. | | `StepperTitle` | `div` | `stepper-title` | Label text for the step. | | `StepperDescription` | `div` | `stepper-description` | Secondary description text for the step. | | `StepperSeparator` | `div` | `stepper-separator` | Connecting line between steps. Changes color when previous step is completed. | | `StepperContent` | `div` | `stepper-content` | Content panel that shows/hides based on whether its step is active. | ### 3. API Design -- Intended JSX Structure **Horizontal layout (typical wizard header with content below):** ```tsx
Account Create your account
Profile Set up your profile
Complete Review and finish

Account creation form...

Profile setup form...

Review and complete...

 ``` **Vertical layout (steps stacked with content inline):** ```tsx
Account Create your account

Account form here...

 ... ``` ### 4. Orientation Handling Following the same approach as `Carousel` and `Tabs`: - **Stepper root**: Sets `data-orientation="horizontal|vertical"` on the root div. Uses conditional classes: - Horizontal: does not impose flex direction on the root (the header row and content area are separate children). - Vertical: `flex flex-col` on the root. - **StepperItem**: - Horizontal: `flex items-center gap-2` (trigger circle + label side by side). - Vertical: `flex items-start gap-3` (trigger circle at top left, labels and content to the right). - **StepperSeparator**: - Horizontal: `h-px flex-1 bg-border` (horizontal line connecting items). - Vertical: `ml-[18px] w-px min-h-4 bg-border` (vertical line, inset to align with trigger center). - **StepperContent**: - Horizontal: content panels sit below the step header row, only the active one is shown. - Vertical: content is nested inside each `StepperItem`, shown only when that step is active. The orientation is available from context, so each sub-component consults `useStepper().orientation`. ### 5. Step State -- Tracking and Styling Each `StepperItem` receives a `step` prop (number). It computes state: ```ts const state = step < activeStep ? "completed" : step === activeStep ? "active" : "upcoming" ``` This state is: 1. Set on the item via `data-state="completed|active|upcoming"` attribute. 2. Provided to children through `StepItemContext`. **Visual states for `StepperTrigger` (the circle indicator):** - **Completed**: Solid primary background, white check icon. Classes: `bg-primary text-primary-foreground`. - **Active**: Primary ring/border, step number displayed. Classes: `border-2 border-primary text-primary`. - **Upcoming**: Muted border, step number in muted color. Classes: `border border-muted-foreground/30 text-muted-foreground`. **Visual states for `StepperTitle`:** - Completed/Active: `text-foreground font-medium`. - Upcoming: `text-muted-foreground`. **Visual states for `StepperSeparator`:** - The separator between step N and step N+1. It receives no `step` prop of its own. Instead, it visually indicates whether the preceding step is completed. The simplest pattern: `StepperSeparator` does not need context -- it uses `data-state` from the preceding `StepperItem` via CSS sibling selectors. However, to keep things explicit and robust (matching the codebase's approach of using context, not CSS tricks), the separator can accept an optional `completed` boolean, or more idiomatically, use a small `step` prop and check `step < activeStep` from context. I recommend: `StepperSeparator` is a purely presentational component. Its parent (`StepperItem` or the consumer) is responsible for placement. It reads `activeStep` from context and does NOT require a `step` prop. Instead, the separator between items visually gets its state from the preceding `StepperItem` using Tailwind's group/peer selectors on `data-state`. But this gets complex. The simplest: the separator does NOT auto-color itself. The demo uses explicit styling OR the separator does nothing smart at all (just a line) and uses `data-[state=completed]` styling passed from context. **Final decision**: Keep it simple. `StepperSeparator` is a dumb line. It supports a `data-completed` attribute and an internal check: if placed between step N and step N+1, the consumer can pass no props and it's just a line. For progress indication, the separator in the recommended demo will be styled via the parent's `data-state` using Tailwind's `group-data-[state=completed]` pattern -- since `StepperItem` wraps its children in a group. Actually, the cleanest approach: do not overcomplicate. The `StepperSeparator` is placed OUTSIDE of `StepperItem` in horizontal mode. So it cannot rely on a parent `StepperItem` group. Therefore, `StepperSeparator` should NOT be "smart" in v1. It is a simple visual connector. The consumer can style it, and in the demo we show a clean look where completed separators are visually distinct by adding a class. But -- to match the "just works" UX expectation, I recommend that `StepperSeparator` accepts an optional `step` prop (defaulting to undefined). When provided, it reads `activeStep` from context and self-applies `data-state="completed"` when `step < activeStep`, enabling styling via `data-[state=completed]:bg-primary`. When not provided, it renders as a plain muted line. This gives both flexibility and convenience. ### 6. Accessibility Following the WAI-ARIA patterns and matching what the codebase does with `Carousel` (role="region", aria-roledescription): - **Stepper root**: `role="navigation"`, `aria-label="progress"` (or user-supplied aria-label). - **StepperItem**: `role="listitem"` inside an `ol` wrapper (or: the step header area is an ordered list). - **StepperTrigger**: `
